sentence: Subjective:

The patient reports being frustrated with her asthma symptoms, stating that she experiences frequent coughing and wheezing, which seems to occur every month or every other month for the past six months.
She mentions that she often visits urgent care and her primary care physician, who may prescribe antibiotics, steroids, or other medications to manage her symptoms.
The patient reports using a blue inhaler, Ventolin, which she finds helpful, but often needs to use it daily.
She has a history of going to the emergency department once as a child due to asthma symptoms.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of asthma, with multiple visits to urgent care and her primary care physician in the past year, including one hospitalization as a child.
Review of systems:
The patient reports no seasonal allergies, but mentions that she occasionally goes to bars where smoking irritates her.
Current medications:
Ventolin (albuterol) 2 puffs every 4-6 hours p.r.n.
Flovent (fluticasone) 110 micrograms 1 puff twice daily
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 128/82
Respiratory rate: 16
Oxygen saturation: 99% on room air
Physical exam:
Diminished lung sounds with occasional slight expiratory wheeze bilaterally
Neck exam: supple, trachea midline, no lymphadenopathy
Cardiac exam: regular rate and rhythm, no ectopic beats, rubs, murmurs, or gallops
Pulmonary function test results: consistent with asthma
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has asthma, moderate to intermediate or moderate intermittent, and a history of multiple acute exacerbations.
Problem list: Asthma; active; confirmed
Plan:
I prescribed Flovent 110 micrograms 1 puff twice daily to help prevent daily symptoms and reduce the need for rescue inhaler use.
The patient can continue using Ventolin as needed for acute exacerbations.
I will also create an asthma action plan to help manage symptoms and prevent future exacerbations.
I would like to see the patient in a month or sooner if symptoms worsen.
Follow-up:
I will see the patient in a month to monitor her symptoms and adjust the treatment plan as necessary.
